company_name: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

company_name: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n

company_name: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
description: Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic

company_name: Stryker says hospital tools are safe, but digital ordering systems still down after cyberattack
description: Electronic ordering systems belonging to the medical device company Stryker are still down a week after a cyberattack believed to have wiped thousands of company devices of all information. The company said its digital products are safe for use.

company_name: Cyberattack disrupts parking payments in Russian city
